info.description: This problem set focuses on fungal pathogens and antifungal agents. The difficulty may vary a lot through out the set, since this set contains a mix of four different cognitive levels of complexity (recall, understand, apply, analyze). The questions are already reviewed, so mistakes should have already been minimized, but if you come across one, you can report through the Forms in the "Info and Links".

q1.prompt: A 40-year-old immunocompromised patient is diagnosed with meningitis caused by *Cryptococcus neoformans*. Which of the following is a recognized virulence factor for this causative agent?

q2.prompt: A 45-year-old patient with onychomycosis is being considered for systemic therapy with an azole antifungal. The physician considered itraconazole but after further examination, it is discarded and replaced with other medication. Which of the following pre-existing conditions is a recognized relative contraindication for this treatment?

q3.prompt: An HIV-positive patient with a CD4+ count of 150 cells/mm^3 presents with a nonproductive cough and bilateral ground-glass opacities on chest imaging. A Gomori methenamine silver (GMS) stain of bronchoalveolar lavage fluid is performed to visualize the suspected causative agent, *Pneumocystis jirovecii*. Which of the following morphological features is characteristic of the mature cyst form in this stain?

options.label: Budding yeast with a narrow base

q4.prompt: A patient from Southeast Asia with AIDS presents with fever, weight loss, and several central umbilicated skin papules. A biopsy of a skin lesion reveals numerous intracellular organisms that replicate by fission. Which of the following is the most likely causative agent?

q5.prompt: A 42-year-old farm worker presents with eye pain, redness, and blurred vision in his right eye after being struck by a plant branch. Physical examination reveals a corneal ulcer. A scraping of the lesion is performed, and microscopic examination shows branching, hyaline, septate hyphae. Which of the following species is most likely the causative agent?

q6.prompt: A patient is prescribed intravenous pentamidine for the treatment of *Pneumocystis jirovecii* pneumonia. Which of the following best describes the mechanism of action for this medication?

options.label: Inhibition of the folic acid pathway
Disruption of fungal cell mitosis by binding to tubulin
Inhibition of DNA, RNA, and protein synthesis
Binding to ergosterol to increase membrane permeability
Inhibition of β-glucan synthesis in the cell wall

q7.prompt: A laboratory technician observes a mold isolated from a diabetic patient with rhinocerebral disease. The microscopic examination reveals hyphae that are broad, irregular, and non-septate, branching at wide angles. These features are most characteristic of which group of fungi?

q8.prompt: Dermatophytes such as *Trichophyton rubrum* are specialized to infect keratinized tissues including skin, hair, and nails. Which of the following enzymes is used by these fungi to invade the stratum corneum by breaking down its primary protein component?

q9.prompt: A patient with cryptococcal meningitis is started on an induction regimen consisting of amphotericin B and 5-fluorocytosine (flucytosine). Which of the following adverse effects is associated with flucytosine therapy and requires close monitoring?

q10.prompt: An agricultural worker in a tropical region presents with small, white, soft nodules that are easily removed from the hair shafts of his scalp. Which of the following organisms is the most likely cause of this condition?
